# Dynamic Decoder Edges: 8-Seed Statistical Confirmation Run

**Date:** 2026-07-16 · **Supersedes** the 3-seed analysis in `2026-07-16_dynedge-multiseed-report.md` · **Data:** `results/seed_stats.json` (per-seed values + paired tests, generated by `analyze_seeds.py`)

## What was run

14 additional 200-epoch runs on thunderlane (4 parallel streams, ~3.8 h wall, all clean):
5 new seeds (7, 13, 99, 2024, 3407) for **baseline** and **dynedge** → n=8 paired
(with existing 1234/1/42), and seeds 1/42 for **full** and **fulldyn** → n=3 paired.

## Headline: the 3-seed effect estimate shrank, the direction survived

| Metric (test unless noted) | Baseline (n=8) | Dynedge (n=8) | paired p (t / Wilcoxon) |
|---|---|---|---|
| ADE (m) | **1.005 ± 0.026** | 1.010 ± 0.032 | 0.62 / 0.95 |
| FDE (m) | **2.846 ± 0.077** | 2.879 ± 0.098 | 0.27 / 0.38 |
| ANLL (nats) | −0.118 ± 0.070 | **−0.162 ± 0.078** | 0.16 / 0.20 (5/8 wins, d=0.55) |
| miss rate | **0.397 ± 0.026** | 0.405 ± 0.015 | 0.38 |
| best val NLL (nats) | −1.52 ± 1.73 | **−2.64 ± 1.99** | 0.17 / 0.25 (6/8 wins, d=0.54) |

- The 3-seed estimate (−2.41 nats val NLL, 3/3 wins) **halved to −1.11 nats, 6/8 wins**
  once five more seeds landed. Losing seeds: 13 and 3407. Baseline val NLL spans
  −4.91…+0.62 across seeds — the variance, not the direction, is the obstacle.
- **Not significant at n=8** (p≈0.16–0.17). Power analysis: at d≈0.54, ~25–30 seeds
  needed for 80% power at α=0.05 (~30 GPU-hours/variant).
- **The point-accuracy null is now strong**: paired ADE diff +0.005 ± 0.027 m (p=0.62).
  This nails H3 (the topology leak was not propping up accuracy) better than before.

## Leak-free variants at n=3 (was n=1)

| Variant | ADE | FDE | ANLL | best val NLL |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| full topo, no feat. | 1.026 ± .03 | 2.900 ± .11 | −0.085 ± .03 | −0.36 ± .52 |
| full topo + dyn. edges | 1.038 ± .01 | 2.949 ± .05 | −0.085 ± .04 | −0.88 ± .70 |

Leak removal costs +0.021 m ADE vs the n=8 baseline (within seed noise) — the
deployability claim stands. Dynamic edges still help leak-free **validation** NLL
(2/3 seeds) but leak-free **test** ANLL is now indistinguishable: H2 is directional
only, weaker than the single-seed picture suggested.

## What changed in the paper

`paper/paper.pdf` rebuilt with all n=8/n=3 numbers, honest significance language
(abstract, Table I/II, Fig. 1b now shows 8 paired seeds, discussion reframed:
deployability first, calibration as "probable but unproven", power analysis in
limitations). The evidence-decay story (3→8 seeds halving the estimate) is
reported explicitly as a methodological caution.

**Conclusion for adoption:** unchanged in practice — dynedge is still the rational
default when covariances feed a planner (positive expected value, bounded downside),
but any public claim should say "direction-consistent, not significant at n=8".
